title: Neon Sprig Lesson Rush
description: Grow a neon sprig by rapidly tapping the right drifting lesson orbs before they fade and confuse what it learns.

howToPlayJson.title: Neon Sprig Lesson Rush
howToPlayJson.objective: Keep the glowing sprig alive through a 3-minute lesson by tapping only the active teachable orbs, chaining correct picks for combo score, and avoiding confusion until the lesson meter fills.

controls.input: Tap a glowing orb that matches the current lesson halo
controls.outcome: The orb pops, the sprig grows, and your combo increases

controls.input: Let a non-matching orb drift away untouched
controls.outcome: You avoid a mistake and keep the sprig stable

controls.input: Tap and hold the sprig with two fingers when the burst ring is full
controls.outcome: All currently correct orbs pop at once for a bonus rescue

steps: A neon sprig sits at the center while moving orbs circle inward from the screen edges.
A clear lesson halo above the sprig shows the current rule, such as 'tap blue code orbs' or 'tap number 3 orbs'.
Tap matching orbs before their glow timer empties; each correct tap adds growth and extends your combo.
Wrong taps or missed matching orbs remove leaves from the sprig; lose all leaves and the run ends.
Every 8 seconds the lesson flips with a loud chime and a screen-edge flash, changing which orbs are safe to tap.
On some flips, a rare Mirror Lesson appears: the halo inverts and you must tap every orb except the highlighted type for a few seconds.
Orb speed, spawn count, and mixed rule complexity ramp up until the lesson ends and your score posts to the local leaderboard.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Correct taps score 10 points times your current combo multiplier. Consecutive correct taps raise the multiplier up to x8. Mirror Lesson orbs give double points. A full bloom burst cashes out bonus points based on how few
tips: Watch the lesson halo first, not the orbit paths; the target rule changes faster than the movement pattern.
Missing a correct orb is safer than tapping a wrong one when the screen gets crowded.
Save bloom burst for Mirror Lessons or late waves when several correct orbs overlap.
Use the audio cues: warm synth notes mean correct lesson orbs, cold clicks warn of wrong taps, and the flip chime signals a new rule.
